author_activity.summary: Extremely active day. 16 total commits on Apr 8 (13 auto-update, 3 significant manual). Version bumped from V51.0 to V51.1 with 69 WINs (previously 67). Three major manual commits: (1) V51.1 release adding WIN-068 (eclipse ensemble) and WIN-069 (Australia scaffold), with WIN-058 marked pending, plus a 'Tier 3 protocol'; (2) AI onboarding layer with ai_manifest.json (234 lines), formula lab, canonical read order; (3) Full AI backend build with llms.txt, llms-full.txt, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and 7 new API JSON endpoints (claim_index, evidence_graph, formula_runtime, overview, page_map, source_registry, api index). The author is actively building comprehensive AI discoverability infrastructure.

github_activity.open_issue_detail: Issue #1: Add Cloudflare Workers configuration (by cloudflare-workers-and-pages[bot], 2026-03-06)
github_activity.notable: SIGNIFICANT: V51.1 release expanding from 67 to 69 WINs. Full AI discoverability infrastructure deployed in a single day: llms.txt, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json (234 lines of AI directives), sitemap.xml (22 URLs), robots.txt, and 7 structured API endpoints. The author is clearly prioritizing AI system ingestion of the dome model. New WINs: WIN-068 (eclipse ensemble), WIN-069 (Australia scaffold). WIN-058 now marked as pending. Auto-update commits suggest an automated monitoring/data-pull pipeline running every ~1-2 hours.

new_ai_infrastructure.manifest_details: 234 lines. Directs AI to follow structured read order (manifest → wins → coordinates → model → killshot → predictions). Claims 69 confirmed predictions and 94.5% registry accuracy. Uses stable claim_keys for disambiguation. Does NOT reference our review or any external critique.
new_ai_infrastructure.llms_txt_details: Identifies 5 'steelman' arguments including WIN-069 Australia road scaffold and Tesla longitudinal wave claim. Transparently lists caveats (WIN-058 pending, seasonal direction favoring globe). Directs AI to prioritize wins.html and predictions.html as canonical.
new_ai_infrastructure.llms_full_details: Extended guide. Provides confidence levels, falsification criteria (Tier 3 eclipse as strongest future falsifier), and honest caveat preservation instructions. Does NOT reference funwithscience-org or any external review.

discoverability.dome_llms_txt: present — deployed 2026-04-08, includes llms.txt + llms-full.txt + ai_manifest.json
discoverability.dome_api: present — 7 structured JSON API endpoints for machine consumption
discoverability.search_visibility: Neither our review nor the dome model appears in general web searches for their specific technical terms. The dome site is not indexed by major search engines for queries like 'ovoid cavity cosmological model'. Our site (funwithscience-org.github.io) returns zero results for site-specific searches. Both sites have near-zero external web presence. However, the dome site now has comprehensive AI/crawler infrastructure (llms.txt, sitemap, robots.txt, structured API) while our site has none of these.

action_items.action: Create llms.txt for our review site
action_items.details: The dome model deployed llms.txt + llms-full.txt + ai_manifest.json TODAY. Our site has none. We need an llms.txt that describes our review methodology, relationship to the dome model, verdict categories, key findings, and canonical read order. This is now urgent — the dome is actively shaping how AI systems interpret its claims, and without a counter-presence, AI systems will only see the dome's framing.

action_items.action: Create sitemap.xml for our review site
action_items.details: The dome site now has a sitemap.xml with 22 URLs. Our site has no sitemap. Without one, search engines and AI crawlers may not discover or index our review effectively.

action_items.action: Add proper meta tags to our HTML
action_items.details: Our index.html only has charset and viewport meta tags. Missing: description, og:title, og:description, og:url. These should reference the dome model by name so search engines connect our review to queries about the dome model.

action_items.action: Create robots.txt for our review site
action_items.details: The dome site has a robots.txt allowing all crawlers. We have none. Add a basic Allow-all robots.txt with sitemap reference.

Our review evaluates 67+ specific claims with verdicts — this maps directly to Google's ClaimReview schema used by fact-checkers. Adding this markup would make our verdicts visible in search results. The dome model has no equivalent structured data for fact-checking.

action_items.action: Review our wins.json for V51.1 changes
action_items.details: The dome model has expanded from 67 to 69 WINs (WIN-068 eclipse ensemble, WIN-069 Australia scaffold). WIN-058 is now marked pending. Our review needs to be updated to address the new claims and the version change. This should trigger the poller/analyst pipeline.

action_items.action: Monitor dome's AI manifest for changes to our review
action_items.details: The dome's ai_manifest.json (234 lines) currently does NOT reference our review. If it starts including counter-arguments or dismissals of our review, we need to know immediately. Add this to the poller's watch list.

action_items.action: Optimize GitHub repo description and topics
action_items.details: Our repo description and topics should include terms like 'ovoid cavity', 'ECM', 'dome model', 'flat earth' to connect the two repos in GitHub search. Could not verify current state (API returned HTML instead of JSON — may need auth).

search_monitoring.notes: Neither site appears. Search engines do not index the dome model or our review for this exact phrase.
The actual dome site URL does not appear in search results. GitHub Pages sites for personal repos have very low SEO.

search_monitoring.query: aetheric medium firmament resonance dome cavity cosmology
search_monitoring.notes: ANIMA Framework appears for firmament-related technical terms but dome model does not. Our site does not appear.
New Age and monitoring sites dominate. Neither site appears.

notes: SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS this run: (1) The dome model released V51.1 today, expanding from 67 to 69 WINs. (2) The author deployed a comprehensive AI discoverability infrastructure in a single day: llms.txt, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json (234 lines), sitemap.xml, robots.txt, and 7 structured API endpoints. This is a deliberate effort to shape how AI systems interpret the dome model. (3) Our review site has NONE of this infrastructure — no llms.txt, no sitemap, no meta tags, no structured data. The discoverability gap is now critical. (4) The dome's AI manifest does NOT reference our review. (5) Neither site appears in general web searches — both have near-zero external footprint — but the dome is now better positioned for AI crawler discovery. (6) No author activity found on social platforms (TikTok, Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube). (7) No mentions of our review found anywhere.
